clusters.title: Parser reports non-worker processes as running workers

member_findings.title: Parser reports non-worker processes as running workers
member_findings.impact: parse_worker_lines only checks that a pgrep result contains -worker_address= and then extracts the address, so concurrent commands such as dscli stop --worker_address=... or any other process with that argument are returned as datasystem workers. Operators can get a wrong status table, and the added test_skips_dscli_process would fail because the dscli line is not filtered out.
member_findings.fix: Apply the declared worker-binary filter before extracting the address, for example skip the line unless _WORKER_BIN_RE.search(cmdline) is true.
